items.title: Weekend Wheel Basics
items.shortDescription: A beginner-friendly intensive covering centering, cylinders, trimming, and finishing.
items.longDescription: Designed for first-time potters, this weekend format combines technical demos with supervised making time. You leave with foundational wheel confidence and multiple finished forms ready for firing.
highlights: All clay, glaze, and firings included

accordionSections.title: Full Description
accordionSections.content: Designed for first-time potters, this weekend format combines technical demos with supervised making time. You leave with foundational wheel confidence and multiple finished forms ready for firing.

accordionSections.title: Highlights
items: All clay, glaze, and firings included

accordionSections.title: Important Information
items: Wear comfortable clothes suitable for studio work.
Please arrive 10 minutes before start time.
Each booking reserves one individual seat.

importantInformation: Wear comfortable clothes suitable for studio work.
Please arrive 10 minutes before start time.
Each booking reserves one individual seat.

editorialSections.title: Guided Studio Flow
editorialSections.body: The session blends concise demonstrations with supervised practice so beginners can quickly gain confidence without feeling rushed.

image.alt: Instructor guiding a student during pottery workshop

editorialSections.title: From Clay To Kiln
editorialSections.body: Participants complete core forms and prepare selected pieces for firing, with glazing support included throughout the weekend.

image.alt: Ceramic pieces prepared for kiln firing
Finished class pieces displayed in the studio
Workshop glaze layering and surface experiments
Refined ceramic forms from open studio session
Blue grey ceramic dinner set arranged on linen

gallery.alt: Hands shaping clay on a pottery wheel during class
Finished workshop work prepared for kiln firing

items.title: Evening Studio Series
items.shortDescription: Build confidence through repeated practice with weekly demos and personalized guidance.
items.longDescription: A progressive series for learners who want consistency. Each week introduces a focused technique while preserving studio time for personal projects and one-to-one feedback.
importantInformation: Suitable for beginners and returning makers.
Each booking reserves one individual seat.

gallery.alt: Weekly class session at the pottery wheel
Collection of finished class pieces on display
Instructor reviewing form and technique with student

items.title: Surface & Glaze Lab
items.shortDescription: Explore layering, wax resist, and texture-driven glaze tests for expressive surfaces.
items.longDescription: An intensive session focused on surface language. We test layered slips, carving, and glaze combinations so you can build repeatable finishing strategies for your own work.
importantInformation: Some prior ceramics familiarity is recommended.
Bring bisque pieces only if already available.
Each booking reserves one individual seat.

gallery.alt: Close-up of glaze layering techniques in workshop
Textured ceramic surfaces showing glaze variation
Surface test pieces staged before firing

items.title: Guided Open Practice
items.shortDescription: Bring your ongoing pieces and receive focused feedback on form and finishing.
items.longDescription: A flexible studio night for returning students and independent makers. Reserve a seat, work on your own pieces, and get targeted guidance where your process needs it most.
includes: Use of shared tools and stations
importantInformation: Best for returning students and ongoing projects.
Each booking reserves one individual seat.

gallery.alt: Open studio practice session on pottery wheel
Instructor discussing form progression with participant
Refined ceramic forms from independent studio practice
